task.title: Prevent cross-layer alignment drift
task.summary: Umbrella sprint for hardening CodeWiki alignment so dogfood state, product source, docs, roadmap, tests, code, validation, commits, and publication cannot drift silently.

goal.outcome: Coordinate the alignment-hardening sprint and close only after child tasks implement executable prevention for vertical drift, horizontal drift, dogfood/source confusion, audit evidence, gateway policy, and publication proof.
acceptance: TASK-078 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-079 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-080 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-081 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-082 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-083 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-084 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-085 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
CodeWiki state is green with no unresolved reconciliation drift after the sprint.
Build, roadmap/task, schema, state, and graph APIs write change_type as canonical metadata.
Readers preserve backwards compatibility for existing change_class builds/tasks where practical.
Canonical change_type values are product, system, task, and code; security/audit/publication/maintenance are not primary type values.
Generated/runtime/mechanical-only changes remain exempt from accepted-build requirements through traceability exemption/non-semantic mode metadata.
Docs, skill guidance, and tests use change_type terminology consistently.
non_goals: Do not treat this umbrella task as implementation evidence for child tasks.
Do not close until child tasks complete or are explicitly deferred with accepted feedback.
Do not force new accepted builds for generated/runtime/mechanical-only graph refreshes.
Do not remove compatibility for existing TASK-082 artifacts if a low-cost alias is possible.
verification: codewiki_state reports green health and no reconciliation drift
All child task verification checks pass

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Implementation validation must not reuse builder thought context and must cite checked content proof.

next_loop.reason: The next gateway must independently validate implementation evidence and cite checked content proof.

summary: Implemented accepted change_type taxonomy migration with compatibility for legacy change_class inputs and traceability exemptions.

evidence: tests/smoke/loop-isolation-policy.test.mjs verifies change_type output, legacy change_class=generated compatibility, and traceability exemption behavior.
tests/smoke/alignment-graph.test.mjs verifies dirty semantic code rows surface change_type=code.
notes: All targeted and full smoke/feature/package checks passed before implementation build.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: src/domain/shared/types.ts defines CHANGE_TYPE_VALUES and TRACEABILITY_EXEMPTION_VALUES with deprecated change_class compatibility aliases.
src/application/builds.ts emits change_type and traceability.exemption, maps legacy change_class values, and preserves accepted-build enforcement for semantic work.
src/application/graph.ts classifies semantic dirty paths into product/system/task/code and reads legacy change_class/exemption metadata.
src/application/roadmap.ts and state-builders.ts write change_type while reading change_class compatibility.
notes: User approved the change_type rename and four-bucket taxonomy as feedback; implementation keeps TASK-077 umbrella open.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: tests/smoke/loop-isolation-policy.test.mjs verifies change_type output, legacy change_class=generated compatibility, and traceability exemption behavior.
tests/smoke/alignment-graph.test.mjs verifies dirty semantic code rows surface change_type=code.
code_change_evidence: src/domain/shared/types.ts defines CHANGE_TYPE_VALUES and TRACEABILITY_EXEMPTION_VALUES with deprecated change_class compatibility aliases.
src/application/builds.ts emits change_type and traceability.exemption, maps legacy change_class values, and preserves accepted-build enforcement for semantic work.
src/application/graph.ts classifies semantic dirty paths into product/system/task/code and reads legacy change_class/exemption metadata.
src/application/roadmap.ts and state-builders.ts write change_type while reading change_class compatibility.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Build, roadmap/task, schema, state, and graph APIs write change_type as canonical metadata.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: types.ts, schemas.ts, builds.ts, roadmap.ts, state.ts, state-builders.ts, and graph.ts now write change_type; smoke tests assert build and graph change_type output.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Readers preserve backwards compatibility for existing change_class builds/tasks where practical.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: builds.ts, graph.ts, roadmap.ts, state-builders.ts, schemas.ts, and deprecated shared type aliases accept/read change_class; loop-isolation smoke covers legacy change_class=generated input.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Canonical change_type values are product, system, task, and code; security/audit/publication/maintenance are not primary type values.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: CHANGE_TYPE_VALUES is product/system/task/code; legacy aliases map security/audit/publication/maintenance/code-bugfix to canonical buckets; docs define labels/profiles/risk metadata for those dimensions.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Generated/runtime/mechanical-only changes remain exempt from accepted-build requirements through traceability exemption/non-semantic mode metadata.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: buildTraceability emits traceability.exemption and semantic=false for legacy generated/runtime/mechanical classes; loop-isolation smoke asserts generated exemption requires no accepted build.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Docs, skill guidance, and tests use change_type terminology consistently.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: .codewiki/kb/system/alignment-model.md, builds.md, compilers.md, audits.md and skills/codewiki loop guidance use change_type terminology.

closure_brief.user_intent: Apply approved TASK-082 follow-up: rename change_class to change_type and simplify taxonomy to product/system/task/code while keeping generated/runtime/mechanical as exemptions.
implemented_changes: Renamed canonical API/build/graph/task metadata to change_type.
Added traceability.exemption for generated/runtime/mechanical and legacy change_class compatibility reads.
Updated docs, skill guidance, and smoke tests.
acceptance_evidence: Smoke and full test suite checks passed.
non_goals_preserved: Did not force accepted-build traceability for generated/runtime/mechanical-only changes.
Did not rewrite old cold artifacts containing historical change_class evidence.
remaining_risks: TASK-077 remains open as umbrella sprint work.

risks: Existing cold artifacts still contain historical change_class prose/fields; readers preserve compatibility instead of rewriting old evidence.

commit.title: chore(codewiki): record TASK-077 Prevent cross-layer alignment drift implementation evidence
commit.body:
Implemented accepted change_type taxonomy migration with compatibility for legacy change_class inputs and traceability exemptions.

Checks: npm run typecheck, node tests/smoke/loop-isolation-policy.test.mjs, node tests/smoke/alignment-graph.test.mjs, npm run test:smoke, npm test, npm run test:features, npm run check:architecture, npm run test:pack
Validation: no durable validation refs recorded.
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
